title: Method calls should not resolve ambiguously to overloads with params
description: Method calls should not resolve ambiguously to overloads with "params"Analyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?The rules for method resolution are complex and perhaps not properly understood by all coders. Theparamskeyword can make method declarations overlap in non-obvious ways, so that slight changes in the argument types of an invocation can resolve to different methods.This rule raises an issue when an invocation resolves to a method declaration withparams, but could also resolve to another non-paramsmethod too.Noncompliant code examplepublic class MyClass { private void Format(string a, params object[] b) { } private void Format(object a, object b, object c) { } } // ... MyClass myClass = new MyClass(); myClass.Format("", null, null); /
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

never_trigger_on: Incompatible parameter types that cannot cause ambiguity
logic.reasoning: This rule uses a custom function that analyzes method overloads to detect potential ambiguous resolution between params and non-params versions. The function: 1) Parses all classes/interfaces and identifies method declarations. 2) Groups methods by name to find overloads. 3) Identifies params versions (with 'params' keyword) and non-params versions. 4) Checks if parameter types could cause ambiguous resolution - where both the params array element type and the non-params version's extra parameters are type-compatible (same type or object). 5) Excludes test classes and cases where types are clearly incompatible.
